references.title: Dialectics After Derrida
references.description: En este articulo se hace una reflexión sobre la relevancia de Derrrida, más allá de las frases de cajón que se le atribuyen. Una de las cuestiones más interesantes que se mencionan, es cómo la
references.reference: A talk by Geoff Boucher... I should look up that guy.

references.title: How To Ask Questions The Smart Way
references.description: Y aunque este artículo está diseñado para la comunidad hacker, creo que refleja la desconexión generalizada entre el público general y la tecnología. Me recuerda cuando era monitor en la universidad, y alguno de mis estudiantes llegaba con preguntas por el grupo de WhatsApp y que la respuesta era literalmente el equivalente de copiar y pegar la pregunta en el buscador de Google, y responder en el chat con el link al primer resultado que frecuentemente era un post de Stack Overflow. Debería poner en mi hoja de vida que sé googlear como Dios manda.

references.title: Quartz
references.description: I really like Obsidian, the graph view and the connectivity dynamics help me a lot in my current thinking process/workflow. And Quartz just let me publish my notes as a website —hostable in GitHub Pages— with the graph view and all the Obsidian goodness. So yeah... NIIIICE.
references.reference: Also... Notes can be public or private.

references.title: On the working definition of Intelligence
references.description: Now that everyone is talking about AI, I think it's important to have a clear definition of what intelligence is. Mainly to avoid the hype and the marketing bullshit. This is a good starting point.

references.title: OliveTin
references.description: Is just a tool I found while arranging some stuff for my university... The outcome of my work was mainly a collection of docker-compose files that would help deploy the infraestrucuture. For non IT people it was a bad idea 🫠. Using OliveTin is helping me give them a good way to interact with the developed infraestructure.

references.title: Burn baby BURN
references.description: Ai toolkit to train AI using RUST 🦀. It's fast and backend flexible)))

references.title: Use an add blocker please
references.description: Like, the blog is really self explanatory... but adds should not be the norm, just google —and similar companies— actually profit out of them; but, because of them, the internet experience is just way worst. So, maybe install an AddBlocker, Please 😸?

references.title: Rust names are important
references.description: I should respect the way it's done.

references.title: 0: Sci Net. (Scientific Communication Support Network)
references.description: Thinking that knowledge is for those who can pay for it, and not for those who need it, is really fucked up. And yeah, this is not free, but not for lack of trying. Sci-Hub made a huge, great impact on open science, and because "free" is usually a bad business model — not for profit, but just for keeping the project running — we now have Sci Net, with crypto, Web3 stuff, and nice things that encourage people to share good knowledge and quality stuff.

references.title: Veilid
references.description: Free software framework aiming to provide encrypted and anonymous communication.
references.reference: If someone to play with this, please let me know... I also want to do it.

references.title: Please, let's just let LinkedIn die
references.description: No one likes it, is really fake, and the worst part, people behave like it matters. And is this behaviour, the generalization of importance of this awful place, what makes it so relevance. So please... let's just let LinkedIn die, once and for all.
references.reference: Link to a great article about it.

references.title: Make youtube great again
references.description: After using this addon/plugin for a while, using youtube without it feels really bad... it sucks for that to be the normal experience for most YouTube users. Only subscriptions, no recomendations, not shorts... JUST GREAT 💞

references.title: De lo real como que queda poco...
references.description: Este fue el mejor lugar para guardar esta canción... luego quiero analizarla más a fondo.

references.title: Intro to Digital Image Processing
references.description: Playlist that I'm using to learn some image processing with gonzalez book in digital image processing.

references.title: 37signals
references.description: I just love how this company works (minus ruby). This home page is art and should be placed in a frame in every business schools.

references.title: The Anarchist Library

references.title: The Pudding
references.description: This is just one of the greatest ways I've seen infromation being showcased. This is just great. So much love, so much effort, this is amazing, the only think I'm going to advice is to whoever look at this, please open the link :D

references.title: OS Data Quality
references.description: By my experience, the thing that makes the worst experience for a developer, that's trying to develop stuff with ai, is the awful quality/quantity of the data available for free... This is just a good blog on how to mantain the quality of the data.

references.title: Deffinition of Slop
references.description: I think is a good idea to have a way to understand what it is; so that is possible to have an acutal understanding of what makes it good or bad. And today, I just found a good definition.

references.title: Crypto & Blockchain Sec. (2022)

references.title: Hacker's Manifesto

references.title: There's way to much entertainment
references.description: If there's a quote I wanna save from this article, it would be: "The human being was not built for this degree of passivity. We are creatures designed for effort, orientation, devotion, memory, craft."
